# Attention: Making the Kernel Learnable {#sec-13-attention}

::: {.callout-warning}
**Chapter stub.** This chapter is planned but not yet written. The abstract below is its
contract; drafting sources are listed for provenance.
:::

*Replace the fixed kernel with learned projections: queries, keys, values. Additive vs
scaled dot-product (and why matrix hardware prefers the latter), the $\sqrt{d}$
scaling, masking, and multi-head attention. The seq2seq bottleneck falls. Harvest two
Chapter 2 seeds by name: softmax as the scores-to-weights machine, and the "softening
the hard" motif — attention **is** the differentiable lookup promised there. Re-run
Chapter 11's leak-free date-normalization benchmark with attention added to the same
architecture and budget: (a) visualize alignment as decoder-step by source-character
heatmaps; (b) compare convergence on the same fixed subset of 400 unambiguous
validation sources. The packed no-attention baseline scores 53.8% at epoch 6 and
95.0% at epoch 12 on that validation subset, then 93.1% in the one final audit on all
437 unambiguous test sources after 25 epochs. Copy `make_date`, the unique-source
rejection, and the 8,000/500/500 train/validation/test split exactly; otherwise it is
not the running benchmark. This is the date-task counterpart of the Chapter 6→8
shift-cliff rematch.*

<!-- draft-sources
seeds:
- `sources/8.1-Attention.tex (rest)`
transcripts:
- `m08 lectures 2-3`
- `[coding]_m08_lecture-8-3-implementing-attention-in-seq2seq-de_nDcFkXXFzJs.txt`
-->
